why_them: Built on Wix Thunderbolt with SEO disabled — a critical flaw for a practice competing in a town with five other dental websites. The homepage renders almost no indexable text content, relying entirely on client-side JavaScript, making it near-invisible to search engines. No email address is publicly listed anywhere on the site. Over 30 years trading, this NHS and private practice is underselling itself badly online.
recommended_service: Website rebuild on WordPress with SEO foundations, local schema markup, and a contact form with email capture
outreach_hook: Your Wix site is essentially invisible to Google — local patients searching for an NHS dentist in Dumfries simply cannot find you.

why_them: WordPress site last meaningfully updated in 2019 — the design is visibly dated with sparse service descriptions and no online booking system across two active surgeries. The practice offers implants, cosmetic work, and dermal fillers yet the website fails entirely to showcase these high-value treatments. Email is JavaScript-obfuscated, reducing accessibility for patients and search crawlers alike.
recommended_service: Website redesign with a dedicated cosmetic and implant services section, online enquiry booking integration, and local SEO
outreach_hook: Your implant and cosmetic services deserve more than a 2019 website — patients searching for those treatments in Annan can't find you.

why_them: WordPress site with a booking button but no email address publicly displayed anywhere. An aggressive chatbot dominates the initial experience and may be driving away anxious dental patients, whom the practice explicitly specialises in treating. Offers implants, clear aligners, and Invisalign but the website undersells these high-margin services with thin content pages.
recommended_service: SEO content expansion for implants and cosmetic treatments, email contact setup, and conversion rate optimisation
outreach_hook: There's no email address on your website — patients looking to enquire about implants or Invisalign have no way to reach you in writing.

why_them: Built on a bespoke jQuery/Modernizr stack that predates modern responsive standards and has no path to an online booking system. The homepage renders contact details poorly with phone number and address buried rather than prominently displayed. Founded in 2010, the clinic offers implants, anti-wrinkle injections, and cosmetic services that are not visibly highlighted for new patients finding the site.
recommended_service: Full website rebuild on modern WordPress with an online appointment request system, cosmetic services showcase, and local SEO
outreach_hook: Your website is built on technology from 2010 that cannot support online booking — you're turning away patients every day who want to self-serve.

why_them: Built on the Duda website builder with significant constraints on SEO customisation and third-party integrations. The site still features COVID-era FAQs, signalling to prospective patients that no one is actively maintaining it. No online booking system exists despite the practice running dental membership plans and serving approximately 3,000 private patients. Team bios and treatment descriptions are minimal for a four-surgery private practice.
recommended_service: Website rebuild with membership plan promotion, online booking integration, and content-led local SEO targeting private dentistry searches in Nithsdale
outreach_hook: Your website still has COVID FAQs on it — a four-surgery private practice deserves a site that actually reflects what you offer today.

why_them: WordPress/Kadence site with integrated booking via Dentr — one of the stronger dental websites in D&G. However, the site has conflicting opening hours information between the footer and schema markup, and treatment pages are underdeveloped with minimal educational content. No patient testimonials carry dates, reducing trust signals. No visible SEO content strategy targeting cosmetic or private dentistry search terms.
recommended_service: SEO content strategy for cosmetic treatments, site audit and schema correction, and conversion-focused landing pages
outreach_hook: Your website shows different opening hours in two places — small errors like that quietly erode trust with patients who are already nervous about dentists.

why_them: WordPress/Elementor site with an appointment request link but no email address listed anywhere, limiting how patients can make contact. The homepage design feels functional but dated with excessive CSS bloat affecting load speed. A four-surgery practice in a prime riverside location offering cosmetic and implant services, but these are not prominently marketed or SEO-optimised to attract private enquiries.
recommended_service: SEO overhaul targeting private cosmetic and implant treatments, email contact setup, and page speed optimisation
outreach_hook: There's no email address anywhere on your website — patients wanting to enquire about cosmetic treatments have no way to contact you except by phone.

why_them: Recently opened NHS and private practice built on WordPress/Elementor but critically incomplete — no phone number is listed anywhere on the site, and the messaging still references an estimated opening date of October 2025, creating serious confusion for prospective patients. Offering 3D scanning, Invisalign, veneers, and composite bonding — a tech-forward practice that needs a professional web presence built from scratch to compete in Dumfries.
recommended_service: Full professional website build with local SEO, complete contact information, NHS registration landing page, and Google Business Profile setup
outreach_hook: Your website still says you're opening in October 2025 and has no phone number — new patients looking to register simply cannot trust or contact you.
